verbGlossary.DEFINES RISK MITIGATION FRAMEWORK FOR: {source}establishes the project's risk mitigation approach, defining action plan structures for safety and quality management before design begins
verbGlossary.SURFACES DESIGN CONFLICTS DURING: {source} phase uncovers clashes or specification contradictions that are formally logged as coordination issues (e.g., architectural details conflicting with structural requirements identified during design review)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS SPEC DEVIATION AT: {source} activity reveals a departure from the approved specification, resulting in {target} report (e.g., design changes during development that deviate from original spec sections)
verbGlossary.BUDGETS CONTINGENCY AGAINST: {source}allocates contingency reserves informed by historical rework cost percentages, using past project data to size the rework allowance in the budget
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES INSPECTION MILESTONES FOR: {source}defines the required {target} hold points that the jurisdiction mandates before work can proceed (e.g., building permit conditions requiring foundation, framing, and final inspections)
verbGlossary.MANDATES SPECIAL TESTING AT: {source}require third-party or special inspections at specific construction milestones (e.g., structural steel moment connections requiring independent weld {target} per building code)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS PRE-MOBILIZATION SAFETY TASKS VI: {source}planning phase creates specific action plan line items for pre-mobilization safety activities such as {target} assessments, JSA development, and PPE procurement
verbGlossary.QUALIFIES SUBCONTRACTORS BY: Bidding and buyout phase uses DART rate history as a qualification criterion when evaluating subcontractor safety performance before awarding contracts
verbGlossary.REQUIRES MATERIAL CERTIFICATION VIA: Submittal review phase demands material test reports and certifications before materials are approved for installation (e.g., concrete mix design submittals requiring cylinder break reports)
verbGlossary.VERIFIED THROUGH SHOP DRAWING REVIEW BY: Submittal phase triggers {target}s when shop drawings are reviewed against design intent (e.g., structural steel connection details checked for specification compliance)
verbGlossary.CATCHES FABRICATION DEFECTS AS: {source}identifies workmanship or dimensional defects before delivery, resulting in {target} reports (e.g., precast panels failing tolerance checks at the fabrication yard)
verbGlossary.CHECKED AT RECEIVING DOCK PER: {source}delivery phase triggers {target} items when goods arrive on site, verifying quantity, condition, and specification compliance (e.g., checking reinforcing steel for mill cert match and damage)
verbGlossary.REJECTS DAMAGED SHIPMENTS WITH: {source}delivery phase generates {target} notices when received goods are damaged, incorrect, or out of specification (e.g., glazing units arriving with seal failures)
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES BASELINE HAZARDS FOR: {source}setup phase generates initial {target}s documenting existing hazards before construction begins (e.g., overhead power lines, unstable adjacent structures, contaminated soil)
verbGlossary.REDUCES FIRST-WEEK INJURIES TRACKED BY: {source}rate of incidents during the critical first week of a worker's presence on site, with effectiveness measured through {target} records
verbGlossary.CALIBRATES REPORTING CULTURE MEASURED BY: {source}sets expectations for near-miss reporting, and the resulting reporting rate serves as a leading indicator of safety culture health
verbGlossary.EXPOSES ABATEMENT VIOLATIONS DURING: Demolition phase uncovers hazardous material conditions (asbestos, lead paint) that result in {target}s when abatement protocols are not followed
verbGlossary.LOGS DEMOLITION HAZARD EVENTS AS: {source}generates incident records when uncontrolled collapses, utility strikes, or other demolition-specific hazards cause {target}s
verbGlossary.REQUIRES COMPACTION TESTING BY: {source}requires third-party geotechnical inspections to verify soil compaction meets design specifications before foundation work proceeds (e.g., Proctor density tests at specified lifts)
verbGlossary.GENERATES SOIL DENSITY REPORTS AS: {source}produce material test reports documenting in-situ soil density, moisture content, and bearing capacity (e.g., nuclear density gauge readings at each compaction lift)
verbGlossary.INSPECTED BEFORE BACKFILL AT: {source}must pass {target} before being concealed by backfill operations (e.g., sewer line slope and bedding inspection prior to cover)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED FOR CONCRETE PLACEMENT AS: {source}are documented through observations recording concrete delivery, placement methods, temperature, and consolidation conditions
verbGlossary.TESTED FOR FLATNESS AND STRENGTH VIA: {source}generates material test reports for F-number floor flatness surveys and concrete cylinder break strength results
verbGlossary.WALKED FOR GRID ALIGNMENT AND INTEGRATIO: {source}is walked and documented through observations verifying grid levelness, module alignment, and proper integration with light fixtures and diffusers
verbGlossary.SNAGGED FOR TILE SEATING AND EDGE CUTS A: {source}generates punch items for acoustical tile seating problems, damaged tiles, misaligned patterns, and rough perimeter edge cuts
verbGlossary.FLAGGED FOR FALL HAZARDS DURING: {source}generates {target}s for fall protection gaps, unprotected edges, and inadequate tie-off points at elevation
verbGlossary.RECORDS IRONWORKER INJURIES AT: {source}captures {target} records for falls, struck-by events, and pinch-point injuries specific to steel erection activities
verbGlossary.CHECKED FOR REBAR PLACEMENT AT: {source}triggers {target} items verifying reinforcement position, cover, and lap splice lengths before deck pours
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS PRE-POUR WALKTHROUGH AS: {source}initiates scheduled inspections (pre-pour walkthroughs) to verify formwork, reinforcement, and embedded items before concrete placement
verbGlossary.CITED FOR GUARDRAIL DEFICIENCIES AT: {source}generates {target}s when temporary or permanent guardrails do not meet height, spacing, or load requirements
verbGlossary.TESTED FOR WATER INFILTRATION VIA: {source}triggers system performance tests (field water spray tests per AAMA 501.2 or chamber tests) to verify weathertightness
verbGlossary.RAISES ANCHOR EMBED CONFLICTS AT: {source}generates coordination issues when anchor embeds conflict with structural elements, slab edges, or other trades' work
verbGlossary.OBSERVED FOR MORTAR JOINT QUALITY DURING: {source}is monitored through {target}s checking mortar joint tooling, head joint filling, and bond pattern consistency
verbGlossary.INSPECTED FOR CONTINUITY AT: {source}requires {target} to verify membrane continuity at transitions, penetrations, and terminations before cladding conceals the work
verbGlossary.FLOOD TESTED FOR WATERTIGHTNESS AS: {source}generates system performance tests through flood testing (ponding water on completed roof sections) to verify membrane integrity
verbGlossary.IDENTIFIES MEP ROUTING CLASHES AT: {source}identifies spatial conflicts between HVAC ductwork, piping, conduit, and structural elements, logged as coordination issues
verbGlossary.VERIFIED FOR DUCT SEALING PER: {source}triggers {target} items verifying duct joint sealing, insulation, and pressure class compliance at accessible rough-in points
verbGlossary.PRESSURE TESTED BEFORE CONCEALMENT AT: {source}requires pressure testing (hydrostatic or pneumatic) and {target} before piping is concealed behind walls or above ceilings
verbGlossary.FLAGGED FOR LOCKOUT-TAGOUT GAPS DURING: {source}generates {target}s when lockout-tagout procedures are not followed during energized work or panel terminations
verbGlossary.REQUIRES HYDROSTATIC TEST BY: Fire protection rough-in phase requires third-party hydrostatic testing of sprinkler piping systems to verify joint integrity and system pressure capacity
verbGlossary.CHECKED FOR CABLE PATHWAY SEPARATION AT: {source}triggers {target}s verifying proper separation between low voltage and power cabling to prevent electromagnetic interference
verbGlossary.VERIFIED FOR STUD SPACING AND BLOCKING A: {source}triggers {target} items checking stud spacing, bridging, backing for fixtures, and header sizes against framing plans
verbGlossary.GENERATES FINISH DEFECT ITEMS AT: {source}produces punch items for visible finish defects such as nail pops, joint banding, cracks, and corner bead imperfections
verbGlossary.GENERATES HIGHEST PUNCH ITEM COUNT AT: Paint and wall coatings phase consistently produces the largest volume of punch items across projects, covering touch-ups, drips, missed areas, and color inconsistencies
verbGlossary.DRIVES FINISH REWORK COSTS MEASURED BY: {source}is a primary driver of finish rework costs, tracked as a percentage of total project cost through the rework cost metric
verbGlossary.PRODUCES ADHESION AND GAP DEFECTS AS: {source}generates punch items categorized by issue type, including tile lippage, carpet seam gaps, VCT adhesion failures, and transition strip defects
verbGlossary.FLAGGED FOR HARDWARE FUNCTION DEFECTS AT: {source}generates punch items for closer adjustment, latch misalignment, threshold gaps, and ADA clearance violations
verbGlossary.NOTED FOR DIMENSIONAL TOLERANCE ISSUES W: {source}generates {target} notices when cabinets, countertops, or trim exceed allowable dimensional tolerances or reveal scribe gaps
verbGlossary.VERIFIED FOR THERMOSTAT CALIBRATION AT: {source}triggers {target} items verifying thermostat setpoint accuracy, sensor calibration, and control sequence response
verbGlossary.SNAGGED FOR LEAK AND ALIGNMENT ISSUES AT: {source}generates punch items for supply connection leaks, drain alignment problems, and fixture mounting defects
verbGlossary.TESTED FOR CIRCUIT CONTINUITY AT: {source}triggers {target} items verifying proper circuit identification, ground continuity, and device function at each outlet and switch
verbGlossary.ACCEPTANCE TESTED FOR ALARM RESPONSE VIA: Fire alarm device installation triggers system performance tests verifying detector sensitivity, notification appliance coverage, and annunciator panel communication
verbGlossary.CERTIFIED FOR SIGNAL INTEGRITY AT: {source}triggers inspections certifying cable performance (Cat6 certification, fiber light levels) and device functionality for data, AV, and security systems
verbGlossary.INITIATES COMMISSIONING OBSERVATIONS AT: {source}testing phase generates the first round of environmental and commissioning observations documenting equipment readiness and pre-start conditions
verbGlossary.COLLECTS ENERGIZATION EVENT TESTIMONY AS: {source}captures witness statements when energization-related {target}s occur, documenting the sequence of events for incident investigation
verbGlossary.GENERATES AIRFLOW BALANCE REPORTS AS: Testing, adjusting, and balancing phase produces system performance test reports documenting airflow rates, water flow rates, and sound levels at each terminal device
verbGlossary.REVEALS SYSTEM IMBALANCE DEFICIENCY RATE: {source}rate of systems failing to meet design airflow or hydronic balance criteria, measured as {target} rate across all tested terminals
verbGlossary.SPAWNS INTERLOCK FAILURE CORRECTIONS FRO: {source}testing phase identifies control sequence failures across interconnected systems (fire alarm to HVAC shutdown, BAS to lighting), generating corrective actions
verbGlossary.ASSIGNS OUTSTANDING DEFICIENCY TASKS FRO: Performance verification phase converts unresolved deficiencies into specific action plan line items with assigned responsible parties and deadlines
verbGlossary.DRIVES RESOLUTION TRACKING THROUGH: {source}is the primary driver of {target} resolution workflows, tracking each item from identification through correction and verification
verbGlossary.MEASURES CLOSEOUT READINESS VIA: Punch list phase uses punch item density (items per unit area or per system) to measure how close the project is to substantial completion
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS TRADE RESPONSE TIME WITH: {source}measures each trade contractor's speed in resolving assigned punch items, benchmarked through {target} closure rate metric
verbGlossary.DETERMINES CERTIFICATE OF OCCUPANCY READ: {source} phase directly determines whether the project can receive a certificate of occupancy based on {target} pass rate across all required disciplines
verbGlossary.CLOSES OPEN OBSERVATIONS FOR: {source}documentation phase triggers closure of remaining open observations as field conditions are recorded and verified against installed conditions
verbGlossary.VERIFIES ALL CORRECTIVE ACTIONS CLOSED A: {source}requires verification that every {target} and action plan has been completed and closed before final payment release
verbGlossary.REOPENS LATENT DEFECTS TRACKED AS: Warranty period phase resurfaces latent defects that were not apparent at substantial completion, tracked as punch item resolutions reopened under warranty provisions
verbGlossary.MONITORS POST-OCCUPANCY SAFETY WITH: {source}tracks recurring {target} patterns (e.g., slip hazards from settlement, mechanical room access issues) using the hazard recurrence rate metric
verbGlossary.VALIDATES SEASONAL LOAD PERFORMANCE VIA: {source}generates system performance tests verifying HVAC capacity and efficiency under actual heating and cooling load conditions across seasons
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS OUTSTANDING ACTIONS DURING: {source}transition phase formally transfers any remaining open corrective actions from the construction team to the owner's facility management team
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES OWNER SAFETY METRICS USING: {source}sets up the baseline safety metrics (TRIR) that the facility management team will track during building operations
